narrative:
The Zurich Zebras enter today’s slate with significant firepower at the top of the order, led by Trea Turner (82.90) and Christian Yelich (81.68). Turner benefits from a massive platoon advantage against Foster Griffin and an elite spot at the top of the lineup, while Yelich leverages a high-ceiling matchup against Nick Martinez bolstered by positive BvP trends. Junior Caminero (77.09) also projects as a standout play, enjoying a favorable platoon split against Kyle Harrison and the premium run-producing potential of the cleanup spot to anchor our 5x5 Roto output.

We face a critical decision regarding the catcher position, where Dillon Dingler (43.70) and Logan O'Hoppe (44.31) both project as bottom-tier starts due to unfavorable lineup positioning and neutral-to-negative matchups. While Josh Smith offers an intriguing 16% park factor boost against Chris Bassitt, owners should monitor the final lineups closely to ensure the projected order holds, particularly for Yordan Alvarez, who faces a 15% penalty due to a same-handed matchup against Ranger Suarez. Leverage the platoon advantages for Pete Crow-Armstrong and Colt Keith to maintain efficiency, but remain cautious of any late-game weather disruptions that could diminish the value of these mid-tier plays.
